title: Fix order cancellation and repositioning logic

requirements.text: Fix the backend order cancellation logic so that when the bot refreshes or stops, all previously placed grid orders are correctly identified and cancelled on ICDex before new orders are placed or the bot halts.

quotes: cancelamento e reposicionamento correto das ordens.

acceptanceCriteria: When the bot stops, all open orders placed by the bot are cancelled on ICDex and none remain open.
When the bot refreshes its grid, existing orders from the previous cycle are cancelled before new orders are placed.
Cancellation failures are logged to the activity log with sufficient detail.
No duplicate or orphaned orders remain after a cancel-and-reposition cycle.

requirements.text: Fix the backend order repositioning logic so that after cancellation, the bot correctly recalculates grid price levels and places new buy/sell orders at the updated positions based on the current market price and configured spread/grid parameters.

quotes: cancelamento e reposicionamento correto das ordens.

acceptanceCriteria: After cancellation, the bot places the correct number of buy and sell orders as configured (numOrders).
New orders are placed at price levels derived from the current mid price and the configured spread.
The activity log records each repositioning cycle with the number of orders cancelled and placed.
If order placement fails for any grid level, the error is logged and the bot continues placing remaining orders.

requirements.text: Update the OpenOrdersPanel and the cancel-all mutation in useQueries.ts so that after a cancel-all action completes, the open orders list is immediately refreshed and the UI reflects the updated (empty or repositioned) order state.

quotes: cancelamento e reposicionamento correto das ordens.

acceptanceCriteria: After clicking cancel-all in OpenOrdersPanel, the open orders list refetches automatically.
The panel shows an empty state or updated orders list once cancellation is confirmed by the backend.
No stale orders remain visible in the UI after successful cancellation.

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo as a single actor.
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
nonGoals: Adding new grid configuration parameters beyond existing spread/numOrders/interval.
Implementing PnL tracking or balance verification.
Changing the visual theme or layout of the dashboard.
